title: AB 1344 reins in predatory out-of-state online colleges by putting students over profits, and AB 807 will help break the cycle of poverty for students who seek higher education

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Online, for-profit institutions based out-of-state have a history of accreditation issues.",
    "Students who attend predatory out-of-state colleges default on student loans at a higher rate than those who enroll at in-state institutions.",
    "In-state institutions operate under significantly higher standards compared to out-of-state colleges.",
    "AB 1344 mandates that out-of-state higher education institutions enrolling California students comply with state accreditation requirements.",
    "AB 807 aims to prevent scholarships from being counted as income when calculating CalWORKs eligibility for low-income students.",
    "The current treatment of scholarships as income discourages families from accepting scholarships.",
    "AB 807 would exclude income earned from assisting in the decennial census from affecting aid eligibility."
  ]
}
```
